rulings.Case Title: ELITISI SEKONA VS. WELLS FARGO HOME MORTGAGE, INC et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 1, 2015, line 2. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT CONTINUED to April 16, 2015. Moving party failed to provide the Court with a copy of the operative pleading. (See SF Local Rule 2.6C.) Plaintiff has also failed to provide the Court with courtesy copies of the opposition. (See SF Local Rule 2.6B.) Courtesy copies must be dropped off in Dept. 501 by 4 p.m. on April 8, 2015 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CONNELLY VS. ELIZABETH MAGUIRE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Special Notice Of And Motion To Strike Complaint [C.C.P. 425.16]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 1, 2015, line 3. DEFENDANTs ELIZABETH MAGUIRE, DANIEL BORNSTEIN and BORNSTEIN & BORNSTEIN's Special Motion To Strike Complaint [C.C.P. 425.16] DENIED. The filing of an unlawful detainer complaint is protected activity and shifts the burden to Plaintiff to present evidence showing a probability of success on the merits of the claim. (See Wallace v. McCubbin (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 1169.) Plaintiff successfully met that burden by showing that he is a prevailing party pursuant to C.C.P. § 1032(a)(4) and presenting evidence regarding acceptance of rent and the continued hostility and ill will between the parties. Additionally, Defendants failed to file the instant motion within 60 days of filing of the operative complaint. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 1, 2015, line 5. DEFENDANT DANIELLE FABIANI'S Motion For Award Of Costs GRANTED. A defendant in whose favor a dismissal is entered or a defendant as against whom plaintiff does not recover any relief is the prevailing party. (Code Civ. Pro., § 1032(a)(4).) Thus, Defendant is the prevailing party here since a dismissal was entered in her favor and she remains in possession of the unit at issue. The costs sought are recoverable under Code Civ. Pro., § 1033.5.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 445 O FARRELL STREET LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. JOSHUA DAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion; Points And Authorities In Suport Of Defendant'S Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment And Recall The Writ Of Possession; Declarations And Exhibits In Support Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 1, 2015, line 8. DEFENDANT JOSHUA DAY'S Motion; Points And Authorities In Suport Of Defendant'S Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment And Recall The Writ Of Possession; Declarations And Exhibits In Support Of Motion GRANTED. Defendant's motion is timely and includes an affidavit showing that Defendant's lack of actual notice was not caused by his avoidance of service or inexcusable neglect and the proposed answer. (See Code Civ. Pro., § 473.5.) = (501/REQ)
